2603.24586.title: Comparing Developer and LLM Biases in Code Evaluation
2603.24586.paper_summary: As LLMs are increasingly used as judges in code applications, they should be evaluated in realistic interactive settings that capture partial context and ambiguous intent. We present TRACE (Tool for Rubric Analysis in Code Evaluation), a framework that evaluates LLM judges' ability to predict human preferences and automatically extracts rubric items to reveal systematic biases in how humans and models weigh each item. Across three modalities -- chat-based programming, IDE autocompletion, and instructed code editing -- we use TRACE to measure how well LLM judges align with developer preferences. Among 13 different models, the best judges underperform human annotators by 12-23%. TRACE identifies 35 significant sources of misalignment between humans and judges across interaction modalities, the majority of which correspond to existing software engineering code quality criteria. For example, in chat-based coding, judges are biased towards longer code explanations while humans prefer shorter ones. We find significant misalignment on the majority of existing code quality dimensions, showing alignment gaps between LLM judges and human preference in realistic coding applications.
2603.24586.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLMs）在程式碼應用中越來越多地被用作評判，它們應該在捕捉部分上下文和模糊意圖的現實互動環境中進行評估。我們提出了TRACE（程式碼評估中的評分標準分析工具），這是一個評估LLM評判者預測人類偏好的能力的框架，並自動提取評分標準項目，以揭示人類和模型在如何權衡每個項目方面的系統性偏見。在三種模式下——基於聊天的程式設計、IDE自動補全和指導式程式碼編輯——我們使用TRACE來測量LLM評判者與開發者偏好的對齊程度。在13種不同的模型中，表現最好的評判者的表現比人類標註者低12-23%。TRACE識別出人類和評判者之間在互動模式中存在的35個重大不對齊來源，其中大多數對應於現有的軟體工程程式碼質量標準。例如，在基於聊天的編碼中，評判者偏向於較長的程式碼解釋，而人類則更喜歡較短的解釋。我們發現在大多數現有的程式碼質量維度上存在顯著的不對齊，顯示出LLM評判者與人類偏好之間在現實編碼應用中的對齊差距。
2603.24586.author: Aditya Mittal et.al.
2603.24586.authors: Aditya Mittal, Ryan Shar, Zichu Wu, Shyam Agarwal, Tongshuang Wu, Chris Donahue, Ameet Talwalkar, Wayne Chi, Valerie Chen
